title: OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF WIC is a public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education classes are provided as part of the program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is not required) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (877) 807-0889 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 673-1222 Main phone (971) 673-1299 FAX 711 TTY/TTD (800) 422-6012 Toll-free WIC Vendor Line (877) 807-0889 Main phone (800) 735-2900 eWIC Card Customer Service (844) 234-4946 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent/guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Vendor Certification WIC WIC Fraud Reporting Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES -
